data.title: Sitewide typography audit + remediation (page-by-page) per DESIGN.md

data.body:
**Umbrella / triage task.** Closes when the audit prompt is authored and per-page sub-tasks are filed — NOT when all per-page work is complete.

## Why

User reviewed the deployed Lab/Publications page (`fractal-nyc.netlify.app/lab`) and identified multiple text elements that are not assigned to canonical type-scale utilities (`.text-display` / `.text-title` / `.text-subtitle` / `.text-body` / `.text-body-lead` / `.text-eyebrow` / `.text-label` / `.text-meta`) and/or violate DESIGN.md guidelines. Examples observed on the Lab page screenshot:

- **Eyebrow** ("RESEARCH + WRITING") — currently italic uppercase mono (?), unclear whether it's using `.text-eyebrow` or ad-hoc
- **Body intro** ("Essays, publications, and podcasts from the minds behind Fractal Labs.") — currently lowercase mono, which conflicts with body tier (Inter, per DESIGN.md FRAC-44)
- **Publication card titles** ("HOW TO LIVE NEAR YOUR FRIENDS") — small, thin, italic Fraunces, all-caps. Doesn't match `.text-title` (3xl/5xl) or `.text-subtitle` (xl/2xl) sizes; probably ad-hoc
- **Author sub-label** ("Priya") — renders the same as body, no semantic differentiation

This pattern is sitewide. The DESIGN.md type scale exists but adoption is partial and inconsistent — FRAC-51/52/54/55/56 migrated headings + eyebrows but did not do a holistic page-by-page audit.

## Approach (two phases)

### Phase 1: Author the audit prompt

A standalone instruction document the per-page sub-agents will follow. Lives at `.lattice/notes/typography-audit-prompt.md`.

The prompt must define:

1. **The canonical type scale** (snapshot from DESIGN.md at audit time — pin the version):
   - Display tier (Fraunces): `.text-display`, `.text-title`, `.text-subtitle`
   - Body tier (Inter): `.text-body`, `.text-body-lead`
   - Chrome tier (JetBrains Mono): `.text-eyebrow`, `.text-label`, `.text-meta`
   - Wordmark carve-out: Jacquard "Fractal" + Fraunces extra-light italic "Collective" — **explicitly out of scope**

2. **A checklist for each text element on a page:**
   - What font-family is it actually rendering? (Fraunces / Inter / JetBrains Mono / Jacquard)
   - What weight, style, transform, size, tracking?
   - Which canonical utility (if any) does it use?
   - Does its rendering match what the utility prescribes? (no override drift)
   - Does its semantic role match the tier? (e.g., is a body paragraph in mono? is a card title in body tier?)
   - If ad-hoc: should it migrate to a canonical utility, OR is there a justified reason it stays ad-hoc?

3. **Decision rubric for findings:**
   - **Migrate**: text fits an existing canonical utility cleanly — replace ad-hoc classes
   - **Justify**: text intentionally diverges (e.g., wordmark, special hero treatment) — record reason in code comment + plan
   - **Flag for system gap**: text needs something the type scale doesn't currently offer (e.g., body-serif tier, small-caps card title) — DO NOT silently invent; surface to orchestrator as a tier-addition decision

4. **What to actually deliver per page:**
   - Audit table: every text element on the page → current rendering → recommended utility → action
   - Plan of edits with exact line numbers
   - Open questions for orchestrator if any system gaps surfaced

5. **Mobile-first requirement:** every audit must read the page at 375px viewport first, desktop second

6. **Explicit non-goals:**
   - Wordmark "Fractal Collective" stays untouched
   - Color / spacing / layout changes are out of scope (typography only)
   - Don't introduce new tiers without orchestrator approval

### Phase 2: Per-page audit + remediation sub-tasks

One Lattice task per page, filed by this umbrella's planner. Each sub-task:
- Links to this umbrella (`lattice link <child> subtask_of FRAC-18`)
- References the audit prompt in its description
- Goes through full Lattice lifecycle (in_planning → planned → in_progress → review → done) with separate sub-agents per stage
- Produces a single PR
- Done sequentially, not in parallel — user explicitly said "audit page by page, not all at once" so the orchestrator should not parallelize these

**Page list** (10 user-facing routes):
1. Home (`/`) → `src/pages/Home.tsx`
2. Story (`/story`) → `src/pages/StoryPage.tsx`
3. Visit (`/neighborhood`) → `src/pages/NeighborhoodPage.tsx`
4. Campus (`/campus`) → `src/pages/CampusPage.tsx`
5. Events (`/events`) → `src/pages/EventsPage.tsx`
6. New Liberal Arts (`/new-liberal-arts`) → `src/pages/LiberalArtsPage.tsx`
7. Political Club (`/political-club`) → `src/pages/PoliticalClubPage.tsx`
8. Publications (`/lab`) → `src/pages/LabPage.tsx` ← **start here; primary motivating example**
9. People (`/people`) → `src/pages/PeoplePage.tsx` (hidden from nav per FRAC-161 but still routable)
10. Protocol (`/the-protocol`) → `src/pages/ProtocolPage.tsx`

Plus the Navbar component itself if any non-wordmark text needs auditing (e.g., the JustifiedParagraph, nav link styles).

## Sequencing

- **Depends on FRAC-17** (typography: drop uppercase from .text-title, drop italic+uppercase from .text-subtitle). The audit prompt needs to reference the *final* canonical spec, not the current one. FRAC-17 must land first or this umbrella's audit prompt will be obsolete on day one.
- Per-page sub-tasks are sequential, not parallel.

## Acceptance criteria for THIS task (triage closure)

- `.lattice/notes/typography-audit-prompt.md` exists, lints clean, reviewed for completeness
- 10 per-page Lattice sub-tasks exist, each linked `subtask_of FRAC-18`, each with a description that references the audit prompt
- Umbrella moves to `done` once both deliverables are in place; per-page work proceeds independently afterward

**SCOPE EXPANDED 2026-06-08.** No longer Fraunces-only — now a full per-page audit covering **all fonts AND color**. Per-page sub-tasks check every text and color use against DESIGN.md and migrate to canonical tokens/utilities.

## Why expanded

User reviewed the Lab/Publications page and observed inconsistencies that cross both typography AND color/token systems:
- Body intro rendered in monospace (font mis-tier)
- Card titles small-caps Fraunces (no matching utility)
- Sub-labels visually indistinguishable from body
- Plus color drift sitewide that hasn't been audited holistically

The audit needs to be holistic per page, not split by axis.

## Audit prompt scope (now covers two axes)

The `.lattice/notes/audit-prompt.md` document will define checklists for both:

### Axis 1: Typography (all fonts, not just Fraunces)

Canonical type scale snapshot (pinned at audit time from DESIGN.md):

- **Display tier (Fraunces):** `.text-display`, `.text-title`, `.text-subtitle`
- **Body tier (Inter):** `.text-body`, `.text-body-lead`
- **Chrome tier (JetBrains Mono):** `.text-eyebrow`, `.text-label`, `.text-meta`
- **Wordmark carve-out (exempt):** Jacquard "Fractal" + Fraunces extra-light italic "Collective"

Per-element checklist:
- Font family rendering — Fraunces / Inter / JetBrains Mono / Jacquard
- Weight, style (italic/upright), transform (case), size, tracking
- Which canonical utility does it use (if any)?
- Does the actual rendering match the utility's prescription? (drift detection)
- Does the semantic role match the tier? (e.g., body paragraph using mono = mis-tier)
- If ad-hoc: migrate / justify / flag-as-system-gap

### Axis 2: Color

Canonical color tokens (pinned at audit time from DESIGN.md):
- Global tokens (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--muted`, etc. from `src/index.css`)
- House tokens (`house-{display-slug}-{light|deep}` from `src/data/houses.ts`)
- Per-page inversion rules (Political Club + New Liberal Arts invert background/accent)

Per-element checklist:
- Color rendering: hex / HSL / Tailwind class / arbitrary value
- Source: canonical token / hardcoded literal / near-duplicate of a token
- Semantic role: surface / text / accent / border / muted / etc.
- Does it respect house scope? (e.g., a Political Club page using house-publications-deep would be wrong)
- If ad-hoc literal: migrate to nearest token / justify / flag-as-token-gap (e.g., near-duplicates like `#f8f6f0` vs `#faf8f5`)

### Decision rubric (both axes)

- **Migrate**: fits an existing canonical token/utility cleanly
- **Justify**: intentionally diverges (wordmark, special hero, etc.) — record reason in code comment + plan
- **Flag for system gap**: needs something the system doesn't currently offer — surface to orchestrator for a tier/token-addition decision, do NOT silently invent

## Per-page sub-task structure

Each sub-task:
- Linked `subtask_of FRAC-18`
- References the audit prompt
- Produces:
  1. Audit table (every text + color use → current → recommended → action)
  2. Plan of edits with exact line numbers
  3. Open questions for orchestrator (system gaps)
- Goes through full Lattice lifecycle with separate sub-agents per stage
- Single PR per page
- **Sequential, not parallel** — user explicit instruction

## Page list (10 routes)

1. Home (`/`) → `src/pages/Home.tsx`
2. Story (`/story`) → `src/pages/StoryPage.tsx`
3. Visit (`/neighborhood`) → `src/pages/NeighborhoodPage.tsx`
4. Campus (`/campus`) → `src/pages/CampusPage.tsx`
5. Events (`/events`) → `src/pages/EventsPage.tsx`
6. New Liberal Arts (`/new-liberal-arts`) → `src/pages/LiberalArtsPage.tsx`
7. Political Club (`/political-club`) → `src/pages/PoliticalClubPage.tsx`
8. Publications (`/lab`) → `src/pages/LabPage.tsx` ← **start here**
9. People (`/people`) → `src/pages/PeoplePage.tsx`
10. Protocol (`/the-protocol`) → `src/pages/ProtocolPage.tsx`

Plus Navbar (non-wordmark text/colors only).

## Non-goals

- Wordmark "Fractal Collective" untouched
- Spacing / layout / animation / interaction — typography + color only
- New tiers/tokens introduced without orchestrator approval
- Parallel execution of per-page work

## Sequencing

- **Hard depends on FRAC-17** (typography spec lock). Audit prompt must reference the final canonical type scale.
- Per-page sub-tasks: sequential, file in priority order (Lab first as primary motivating example, then likely Home / Campus / NLA / Story / others as user wants).

## Acceptance criteria for THIS umbrella (triage closure per memory rule)

- `.lattice/notes/audit-prompt.md` exists, complete for both typography + color axes
- 10 per-page sub-tasks filed and linked `subtask_of FRAC-18`
- Each sub-task description references the audit prompt
- Umbrella → `done` when both deliverables are in place; per-page work proceeds independently

**SCOPE REVISION 2026-06-08 — fully autonomous, no human-in-the-loop.**

User intent: this should be a "kick it off and walk away" pipeline. No orchestrator gates that require human decisions. Once FRAC-17 lands, the umbrella runs the full audit + per-page remediation end-to-end without intervention. The forward goal: once every text/color use is bound to a canonical utility/token, the user can later say "make the eyebrow thinner" and a single change to `src/index.css` + DESIGN.md propagates sitewide.

## Key shifts from the prior plan

### 1. Audit prompt must be PRESCRIPTIVE, not just descriptive

Every possible finding gets a deterministic action. The planner never asks the orchestrator a question.

### 2. System-gap handling is non-blocking

Previously: "flag to orchestrator, await tier-addition decision."
Now: **always migrate to the nearest-fit canonical utility/token, and append the gap to a sitewide register** at `.lattice/notes/audit-gaps.md` for later batch review by a human.

The audit-gaps register is the ONLY persistent human-facing artifact from the audit. It collects:
- Element location (file:line)
- What didn't fit and why
- Which nearest-fit utility/token was used
- One-line proposed system change (e.g., "add `.text-card-title` tier" or "add `--accent-pink-deep` token")

The page PR ships with the nearest-fit migration. Human reviews the gaps register at their leisure and decides whether to add tiers/tokens.

### 3. No `needs_human` transitions during per-page work

Planner never moves a sub-task to `needs_human`. If the planner is genuinely stuck (e.g., page won't compile to read), it bubbles up as a comment on the umbrella and moves to `needs_human` at the umbrella level — never at the per-page level.

### 4. Same-PR-per-page (audit + implementation together)

Plan file = audit report. Implementation sub-agent reads the plan, executes, opens one PR. Review sub-agent checks diff against plan and acceptance criteria. Done autonomously.

### 5. Orchestrator drives sequence

The umbrella's orchestrator script:
1. Wait for FRAC-17 to merge
2. Author `.lattice/notes/audit-prompt.md` (Phase 1)
3. File 10 per-page sub-tasks linked `subtask_of FRAC-18`
4. For each sub-task in priority order (Lab first):
   - Spawn planner → planned
   - Spawn impl → review
   - Spawn reviewer → done (or rework loop per Lattice 3-cycle rule)
   - Open PR, await merge, advance to next page
5. After last page, close umbrella

## Updated audit-prompt scope (must be prescriptive)

For each text element on a page, the planner answers:

```
Element: <file:line, surrounding context>
Current: family=X, weight=Y, style=Z, transform=W, size=S, tracking=T
Nearest canonical utility: <one of .text-display/.text-title/.text-subtitle/.text-body/.text-body-lead/.text-eyebrow/.text-label/.text-meta>
Match quality: EXACT | NEAR | GAP
Action: MIGRATE | JUSTIFY-WORDMARK | GAP-LOG-AND-MIGRATE
Rationale: <one line>
```

For each color use:

```
Element: <file:line>
Current: <hex | hsl | Tailwind class | arbitrary>
Nearest canonical token: <global token or house-{slug}-{light|deep}>
Match quality: EXACT | NEAR | GAP
Action: MIGRATE | JUSTIFY | GAP-LOG-AND-MIGRATE
Rationale: <one line>
```

The audit table goes verbatim into the plan file. The gaps (`Match quality: GAP`) ALSO get appended to `.lattice/notes/audit-gaps.md` so they accumulate sitewide.

## Acceptance criteria for THIS umbrella (unchanged closure rule)

- `.lattice/notes/audit-prompt.md` exists and is prescriptive
- `.lattice/notes/audit-gaps.md` exists (starts empty, populates as audits run)
- 10 per-page sub-tasks filed and linked `subtask_of FRAC-18`
- Umbrella → `done` after triage complete (per memory rule: umbrella closes when triage is done, NOT when all children finish)

## Acceptance criteria per per-page sub-task

- Plan file contains the full audit table
- All MIGRATE and GAP-LOG-AND-MIGRATE actions executed in the PR
- New gaps appended to `.lattice/notes/audit-gaps.md`
- `pnpm type-check`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm test` pass
- Visual at 375px viewport confirmed by impl agent
- PR opened, single commit per logical group

## Non-goals (unchanged)

- Wordmark "Fractal Collective" untouched
- Spacing / layout / animation
- Introducing new tiers/tokens (those go into the gaps register, not into the page PR)

## The future-state payoff

Once the audit completes, every text/color reference in the codebase is bound to a canonical utility/token. The user can then say things like:

- "make the eyebrow thinner" → single change to `.text-eyebrow` in `src/index.css` + DESIGN.md, propagates sitewide
- "darken the cream background" → single change to `--background` token
- "drop the title tracking" → single change to `.text-title`

That propagation is the whole reason for doing this audit.

**SCOPE REVISION 2026-06-08 — per-page audit and update are SEPARATE Lattice tasks → separate PRs.**

User direction: each Lattice task = one PR ticket. Auditing a page and updating a page are two distinct contexts; bundling them into one task makes the context too big (some pages are 700+ lines, with 30+ elements to audit and 20+ edits to make). Splitting keeps each sub-agent's job lean and focused.

## Revised child-task structure under FRAC-18

```
FRAC-18 (umbrella)
├── 18.A  Author audit prompt + gaps register             → 1 PR
├── 18.B  Audit Lab page (typography + color)             → 1 PR (doc only)
├── 18.C  Apply Lab page audit findings                   → 1 PR (code changes)
├── 18.D  Audit Home page                                 → 1 PR (doc only)
├── 18.E  Apply Home page audit findings                  → 1 PR (code changes)
│  ... (pattern repeats for each page)
└── 18.U  Apply Protocol page audit findings              → 1 PR (code changes)
```

**Total child PRs: 1 prompt-authoring + 10 audit + 10 update = 21 PRs across 21 Lattice sub-tasks.**

## What each task type does

### Audit task per page (no source changes)

- Reads `.lattice/notes/audit-prompt.md`
- Reads the page source + its component tree
- Writes audit table to `.lattice/notes/audits/<page-slug>-audit.md`
- For each finding: classifies as MIGRATE / JUSTIFY-WORDMARK / GAP-LOG-AND-MIGRATE
- Appends any gaps to `.lattice/notes/audit-gaps.md`
- PR ships: only the audit markdown file(s)
- Lifecycle: planner → impl (writes the doc) → reviewer (checks doc completeness against prompt rubric) → done

### Update task per page (only source changes)

- Reads the prior page's audit doc at `.lattice/notes/audits/<page-slug>-audit.md`
- Executes every MIGRATE and GAP-LOG-AND-MIGRATE action with exact line-number edits from the audit
- No re-auditing, no decisions — just executes what the audit prescribed
- PR ships: only source code changes (no audit doc edits)
- Lifecycle: planner (reads audit, writes implementation plan w/ exact edits) → impl → reviewer (diff vs audit) → done
- Strict tests pass + 375px visual confirmation

## Why split

- Each sub-agent gets a single focused responsibility — better outcomes than asking one to do both
- Audit PR is reviewable as a markdown diff (cheap to scan)
- Update PR is reviewable as a code diff against an explicit spec (the audit doc)
- If the audit is wrong, it's caught before any code changes
- Smaller context per task → faster + more reliable agent execution
- Each PR has a single coherent purpose

## Sequencing (autonomous orchestrator script)

After FRAC-17 merges:

1. Run 18.A (author audit prompt) → merge
2. For each page in priority order (Lab first):
   - Spawn audit task → planner → impl → reviewer → PR opens → merge
   - Spawn update task → planner → impl → reviewer → PR opens → merge
3. Audit-gaps register grows over time; user reviews it at their leisure
4. Close umbrella once all 21 children are done OR triage shows the structure is in place (per memory rule: umbrella closes when triage is complete)

## Priority order (suggested)

1. Lab/Publications ← primary motivating example
2. Home
3. Campus
4. Story
5. New Liberal Arts
6. Political Club
7. Visit (Neighborhood)
8. Events
9. People
10. Protocol

Each pair (audit, update) is sequential within the page; pages are also sequential (no parallel page work, per prior decision).

## Non-goals (unchanged)

- Wordmark "Fractal Collective" untouched
- Spacing / layout / animation
- No new tiers/tokens silently introduced — they go to the gaps register only

## Acceptance criteria for THIS umbrella (triage closure)

- 18.A complete (audit prompt + gaps register in place)
- 20 per-page sub-tasks filed (10 audit + 10 update) under FRAC-18
- Each pair linked + sequenced
- Umbrella closes when triage structure exists; per-pair execution proceeds autonomously

**Note on child-task IDs (2026-06-08):**

Prior comments used illustrative IDs like "18.A", "18.B", etc. to show grouping. Those are NOT real Lattice codes. Lattice issues sequential FRAC-N at creation time.

When the umbrella's planner files child tasks, expect:

```
FRAC-18  (umbrella, this task)
├── FRAC-<n+1>   Author audit prompt + gaps register   (one-off setup)
├── FRAC-<n+2>   Audit Lab page                        (pair 1 — audit)
├── FRAC-<n+3>   Apply Lab page audit                  (pair 1 — update)
├── FRAC-<n+4>   Audit Home page                       (pair 2 — audit)
├── FRAC-<n+5>   Apply Home page audit                 (pair 2 — update)
...                                                    (21 children total)
```

Linking convention (apply to every child):
```
lattice link <child-FRAC-N> subtask_of FRAC-18 --actor agent:claude-opus-4-7-orchestrator
```

Page-pair naming convention for each title (for scannability in `lattice list`):
- `Audit <Page name> page (typography + color)`
- `Apply <Page name> page audit findings`

The orchestrator script should: file the setup task first, then for each of the 10 pages file the audit task + update task as a pair, link each to FRAC-18, and add an explicit `depends_on` link from each update task to its corresponding audit task:

```
lattice link <update-FRAC-N> depends_on <audit-FRAC-N> --actor ...
```

This makes the dependency machine-readable: the update task can't move out of `backlog` until its audit task is `done`.
